title: Universal Immunization Programme (UIP)
focus: Coverage, achievements, and digitalization of India's Universal Immunization Programme.
rationale: UIP is a core public health initiative in India. Questions can target the specific diseases covered, the U-WIN platform, and its integration with the National Rural Health Mission.
sectionExcerpt: Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) of India Provides free immunization against 12 diseases: Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus, Polio, Measles, Rubella, Childhood Tuberculosis, Hepatitis B, Meningitis & Pneumonia, Rotavirus diarrhoea, Pneumococcal Pneumonia, and Japanese Encephalitis. Under the National Rural Health Mission, the UIP has become a central component of India's public health efforts. Achievements: Polio-Free India and Neonatal tetanus elimination. U-WIN Platform has been launched for the digitalization of all vaccination services provided under UIP.
evidenceBullets: UIP provides free immunization against 12 diseases including Diphtheria, Polio, Measles, Rubella, Rotavirus, Pneumococcal Pneumonia, and Japanese Encephalitis.
The programme operates as a central component under the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM).
Key achievements of the UIP include making India Polio-Free and eliminating Neonatal tetanus.
The U-WIN Platform was launched to digitalize all vaccination services provided under the UIP.

title: Monoclonal Antibodies (mAbs) and Nipah Virus
focus: Definition, origin, and application of monoclonal antibodies, specifically regarding the Nipah virus.
rationale: Biotechnology and disease treatment are high-yield UPSC topics. The mechanism of mAbs and their use against zoonotic diseases like Nipah are highly testable.
sectionExcerpt: The Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) seeks partners to develop monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) against Nipah viral disease. Nipah virus is a zoonotic pathogen causing severe respiratory and neurological illness, transmitted from bats or infected animals to humans. Monoclonal antibodies are laboratory-produced proteins, engineered to bind specific antigens, mimicking natural immune responses. Origin: Derived from identical immune cells cloned from a single parent B-cell.
evidenceBullets: ICMR is seeking partners to develop monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) against the Nipah virus.
Nipah virus is a zoonotic pathogen transmitted from bats or infected animals to humans, causing severe respiratory and neurological illness.
Monoclonal antibodies are laboratory-produced proteins engineered to bind specific antigens, mimicking natural immune responses.
mAbs are derived from identical immune cells cloned from a single parent B-cell and are used to treat cancers, autoimmune disorders, and viral infections.

title: WHO 3 By 35 Initiatives and Health Tax
focus: WHO's initiative to tax unhealthy products to combat non-communicable diseases.
rationale: Global health initiatives and taxation policies for public health are relevant for governance and health sections.
sectionExcerpt: WHO launched 3 By 35 Initiatives, aims to raise prices of any or all of three unhealthy products tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% through health taxes by 2035. What is Health Tax? Levied on products that have a negative public health impact e.g. Tobacco, etc. WHO recommends taxation as one of the most cost-effective tools for addressing population levels of obesity and other related non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
evidenceBullets: WHO's 3 By 35 Initiative aims to raise prices of tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% by 2035.
Health taxes are levied on products with negative public health impacts to address obesity and non-communicable diseases (NCDs).

title: National One Health Mission
focus: Integration of human, animal, and environmental health under the National One Health Mission.
rationale: The 'One Health' approach is a major paradigm shift in public health, frequently emphasized in recent policies to combat zoonotic diseases and antimicrobial resistance.
sectionExcerpt: Union Government will launch National One Health Mission (NOHM). One Health is an integrated, unifying approach to balance and optimize the health of people, animals and the environment. A multi-sectorial initiative integrating human, livestock, wildlife and environmental health to strengthen coordinated surveillance, diagnostics and outbreak response. Nodal Agency: Anchored by Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) under Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser (PSA). Anchor Institution: National Institute of One Health, Nagpur.
evidenceBullets: NOHM is a multi-sectorial initiative integrating human, livestock, wildlife, and environmental health.
The mission is anchored by the ICMR under the Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser (PSA).
The Anchor Institution for the mission is the National Institute of One Health, located in Nagpur.
The mission addresses needs such as Zoonotic Risk Mitigation, Pandemic Preparedness, and curbing Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR).

title: Trans Fats and Dietary Fats
focus: Health impacts of trans fats, WHO's REPLACE package, and India's FSSAI regulations.
rationale: Nutrition and food safety regulations, particularly FSSAI limits and WHO guidelines, are standard UPSC science and health topics.
sectionExcerpt: The WHO officially recognized four countries (Austria, Norway, Oman and Singapore) for their outstanding leadership in eliminating industrially produced trans fats. TFA are unsaturated fatty acids that can be industrially produced (via hydrogenation) or naturally found in animal-source foods. Raises the level of the bad cholesterol and low density lipoprotein-cholesterol (LDL-c). WHO's REPLACE Action Package. India enforced limiting trans fats in oils to no more than 2 % through the Food Safety and Standards Second Amendment Regulations, 2021.
evidenceBullets: Trans-fatty acids (TFA) are unsaturated fatty acids produced industrially via hydrogenation or found naturally in animal-source foods.
TFAs raise bad cholesterol (LDL-c) and are linked to inflammation, obesity, diabetes, and high blood pressure.
WHO's REPLACE Action Package aims to eliminate industrially produced trans fats globally.
India's FSSAI enforced a limit on trans fats in oils to no more than 2% in 2021.

title: Weight Loss Drugs and GLP-1 Agonists
focus: Mechanism of action of new weight loss drugs like Wegovy and their interaction with GLP-1.
rationale: The mechanism of GLP-1 agonists is a recent breakthrough in medical science, making it a prime candidate for Science & Tech questions.
sectionExcerpt: Danish pharma company Novo Nordisk launched its weight loss drug Wegovy in India. The drug semaglutide, marketed under the brand name Wegovy, will be available in the form of injections. Semaglutide, works as an appetite suppressant by imitating a hormone called glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1). GLP-1 is an intestinal hormone released after eating, and usually makes people feel fuller.
evidenceBullets: Wegovy (semaglutide) is a weight loss drug administered via injections.
Semaglutide acts as an appetite suppressant by imitating the intestinal hormone glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1).
GLP-1 is naturally released after eating and makes people feel fuller.
Other weight loss drugs like Mounjaro use tirzepatide, which mimics both GLP-1 and GIP functions.

title: Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS)
focus: Composition, function, and FSSAI directives regarding WHO-recommended ORS.
rationale: ORS is a fundamental public health tool. Its exact WHO composition and the recent FSSAI directive against misleading marketing are highly testable.
sectionExcerpt: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has directed states and UTs to remove beverages and similar products being marketed using the term ORS. ORS term is associated exclusively with WHO-recommended Oral Rehydration Salts, which are drug products. WHO recommended ORS salts composition: Sodium chloride, glucose (anhydrous), potassium chloride, and trisodium citrate (dihydrate). Functions: Glucose enhances sodium and water absorption, sodium and potassium replace essential electrolyte losses, and citrate helps correct dehydration-related acidosis.
evidenceBullets: FSSAI directed the removal of commercial beverages misleadingly marketed using the term ORS.
WHO-recommended ORS composition includes Sodium chloride, anhydrous glucose, potassium chloride, and trisodium citrate dihydrate.
In ORS, glucose enhances sodium and water absorption, while citrate corrects dehydration-related acidosis.
ORS was developed by Dilip Mahalanabis as a low-cost solution to treat dehydration from acute diarrhoeal diseases.
